title: Course: COSC 458 - Software Engineering
description: Covers fundamental software engineering concepts including feasibility study, development, testing, deployment, and maintenance phases following standard SDLC. Topics include formal methods, re-engineering, reverse engineering, version control, reliability, risks, software ethics, project management, and team organization. Students complete a large team-based software project.
prerequisites_text: COSC 220 completed with a grade of C or higher
content:
COSC 458 - Software Engineering
Credits: 3
Prerequisites: COSC 220 completed with a grade of C or higher
Offered: Fall, Spring

Description: Covers fundamental software engineering concepts including feasibility study, development, testing, deployment, and maintenance phases following standard SDLC. Topics include formal methods, re-engineering, reverse engineering, version control, reliability, risks, software ethics, project management, and team organization. Students complete a large team-based software project.

Curriculum role:
  - BS Computer Science: Core
  - BS Cloud Computing: Group C

Catalog page: https://catalog.morgan.edu/preview_course_nopop.php?catoid=26&coid=55601
